title: Government Systems & Targeted Attacks
description: Test your understanding of why local governments are targeted and how employees should respond to warning signs.

questions.prompt: Why are local governments attractive targets for cyber attackers?

choices.text: Because they do not provide important services
Because they often manage essential services and sensitive information
Because only federal agencies are attacked
Because local governments never use technology

questions.explanation: Local governments often support critical services and hold operational or sensitive information that attackers may want to disrupt or access.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is one possible goal of a targeted attack against a government agency?

choices.text: To improve the agency's website
To disrupt services, steal information, or demand payment
To reduce paperwork
To help employees log in faster

questions.explanation: Attackers may target government agencies for money, disruption, intelligence, or long-term access.

questions.prompt: What does the term Advanced Persistent Threat (APT) generally mean?

choices.text: A software patch that improves performance
A highly organized attacker that may try to stay hidden inside systems over time
A required yearly compliance test
A public alert system for residents

questions.explanation: APT generally refers to organized attackers that may quietly maintain access for long periods.

questions.prompt: Cybersecurity is only the responsibility of technical staff.

choices.text: True
False

questions.explanation: All employees play a role in reducing risk, especially by recognizing and reporting suspicious activity.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a warning sign that should be reported?

choices.text: Unexpected password reset notifications
Suspicious urgent requests for sensitive information
Login alerts from unfamiliar locations
All of the above

questions.explanation: These are all examples of warning signs that could indicate suspicious or malicious activity.

questions.prompt: Why can cyber incidents affect public trust in a government agency?

choices.text: Because residents may lose confidence when services are disrupted or data is exposed
Because cyber incidents automatically erase all records
Because only IT notices them
Because public trust is unrelated to service availability

questions.explanation: Residents depend on government services, and disruptions or data exposure can reduce confidence in the agency.

questions.prompt: Scenario: You receive a message claiming to be from agency leadership asking you to urgently send internal information to a new external address. What should you do first?

choices.text: Send the information quickly because it appears urgent
Reply to the message and ask if it is real
Verify the request through a trusted channel and report it if suspicious
Forward it to coworkers and ask them to decide

questions.explanation: Urgent requests for sensitive information should be verified through trusted channels and reported if suspicious.

questions.prompt: What should an employee do after noticing strange system behavior or a suspicious request?

choices.text: Ignore it unless systems completely fail
Try to investigate everything alone
Report it through the agency's process and follow guidance
Post about it publicly first

questions.explanation: Prompt reporting and following agency procedures helps reduce damage and supports a coordinated response.
